articles.title: Kylian Mbappe, France put on a show at Gillette Stadium World Cup preview, beating Brazil in star-studded friendly
articles.description:
The victory extended France’s unbeaten streak to eight matches, dating to a June loss to world No. 1 Spain in the Nations League.

The post Kylian Mbappe, France put on a show at Gillette Stadium World Cup preview, beating Brazil in star-studded friendly appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: France defeated Brazil 2-0 in a friendly match at Gillette Stadium.
Kylian Mbappe showcased his skills, contributing to France's eight-match unbeaten streak.
The event attracted local fans, boosting community engagement and interest in the upcoming World Cup.

articles.title: French squad gets introduced to Foxborough game-day traffic jams, and setting for this summer’s World Cup
articles.description:
France defeated Brazil, 2-1, in a friendly match before a crowd of 66,215 Thursday, less than three months before the World Cup kickoff.

The post French squad gets introduced to Foxborough game-day traffic jams, and setting for this summer’s World Cup appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: France defeated Brazil 2-1 in a friendly match at Gillette Stadium.
66,215 fans attended the match, highlighting local interest in soccer.
World Cup preparations may increase traffic congestion for Boston residents this summer.

articles.title: He’s running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children’s Hospital
articles.description:
The 2026 Boston Marathon marks a decade of running for Jace Perkins, once a 1.6-pound NICU baby, now healthy and thriving.

The post He’s running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children’s Hospital appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jace Perkins will run his 11th Boston Marathon on April 20, 2026.
Perkins, once a 1.6-pound NICU baby, supports Boston Children's Hospital.
His story highlights the hospital's impact on local families and infant care.

articles.title: Today’s solo home buyers have something in common: They’re mostly women
articles.description:
Where three solo female buyers landed in the Boston area.

The post Today’s solo home buyers have something in common: They’re mostly women appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Solo female buyers dominate the Boston housing market, reflecting changing demographics.
Three women recently purchased homes in Boston, highlighting a trend in solo home ownership.
This shift may influence future housing policies and market dynamics in the Boston area.
